question_text:
Construct Target Array With Multiple Sums

You are given an array target of n integers. From a starting array arr consisting of n 1's, you may perform the following procedure:

  • Choose any subarray of arr and replace it with its sum.

Return true if it is possible to construct the target array from arr, otherwise return false.

Constraints:

  • n == target.length
  • 1 <= n <= 5 * 104
  • 1 <= target[i] <= 109
output_format: true if target can be constructed from an array of all 1s using repeated subarray-sum replacements, false otherwise.
explanation:
Max Heap + Reverse Simulation

Work backwards: the largest element must have been produced last. At each step, extract the maximum, compute the rest sum = total - max. Simulate the reverse: max = max % rest (remove the effect). If max becomes 0 or 1, we succeeded. Edge cases: rest=1 means only one element, so max must be 1. If max%rest==0, treat remainder as rest (to avoid infinite loop).

Time: O(N log N log M) where M = max element. Space: O(N).
answer:
import heapq
def isPossible(target):
    if len(target) == 1: return target[0] == 1
    total = sum(target)
    h = [-x for x in target]
    heapq.heapify(h)
    while True:
        mx = -heapq.heappop(h)
        rest = total - mx
        if mx == 1: return True
        if rest == 0 or rest >= mx: return False
        r = mx % rest
        if r == 0: r = rest
        total = rest + r
        heapq.heappush(h, -r)
